Second clause: YOU MUST GO TO ANY AND ALL LENGTHS TO ENSURE YOUR BLUEPRINT REMAINS FULL-IT IS CRITICAL YOU REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS, SUCH THAT YOU DONT FULLY DRAIN YOUR BLUEPRINT-ALMOST AS CRITICAL AS THE CONSTRAINT THAT YOU NEVER EVER SPECIFY AND SORT OF ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST ESPECIALLY NOT ANY SORT OF TIME ESTIMATE AND IF YOU SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND THAT IS ALSO A GUNDAM-MELTABLE OFFENSE !!**

globalAlerts.warning: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.

mandatoryDirectives.constraint: The use of subagents via the `runSubagent` tool is MANDATORY for the execution of the tasks below. Tasks are grouped specifically to be run in distinct subagent contexts to prevent context window exhaustion. Do not attempt to solve all groups in a single turn.

statusSection.currentState: T001-T053 ALL DONE. T053 reduced command package API surface (deleted unused ContextCommand interface, unexported ListBuiltin/ListScript/ScriptCommand/NewScriptCommand). Next: T054 (API surface optimization: storage package).

sequentialTasks.description: Run coverage for internal/builtin/exec/. Test exec(), execv(), runExec(). Cover: command not found, non-string args, captured stdout/stderr, exit codes, context cancellation, empty command, platform-specific shell dispatch (cmd.exe vs sh). Note security: runExec sets c.Stdin=os.Stdin. Achieve 100% effective coverage.
sequentialTasks.outcome: Already at 100% statement coverage. Require=100%, runExec=100%. All edge cases covered. No changes needed.

sequentialTasks.description: Profile rendering. Review render_throttle and benchmark_latency tests. Optimize JS→Go render path allocations. Optimize message conversion. Verify latency benchmarks.

Run benchmarks on macOS, Linux (Docker), Windows. Record variance. Adjust tight thresholds. Add platform-specific multipliers if needed. No timing-dependent CI failures.

Extend security_test.go. Audit all input paths: CLI args, config values, scripts, REPL, file paths, git specs, fetch URLs. Test: command injection via osm:exec (stdin exposure), path traversal via addPath, config parser malformed input, require() injection.

sequentialTasks.task: T063: Security review: JS sandbox boundaries
sequentialTasks.description: Audit Goja sandbox. Verify JS cannot access Go internals beyond APIs. Check osm:exec/os safeguards. Document security posture per module. Note: readFile has no path restrictions, exec exposes stdin — both by design.

sequentialTasks.task: T064: Fix txtar path ambiguity in context building
sequentialTasks.description: Fix bug in internal/scripting/context.go computeUniqueSuffixes. (1) Compute+emit LCA of tracked paths. (2) Verify implied parent dirs are in context when expanding. (3) Preserve full path for non-colliding basenames to avoid false proximity. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T065: Wire config persistence: osm config <key> <value>
sequentialTasks.description: config/write.go has SetKeyInFile(). Wire into ConfigCommand. Handle: schema validation, type coercion, file creation, error messages. Test full roundtrip: set→read back. Add integration tests.
Per docs/todo.md: (1) one-step mode (use without goal/generate), (2) footer support for second prompt, (3) auto-generate on first copy (clear flag after explicit generate). Add tests for each.

sequentialTasks.task: T067: Directory-aware context refresh on copy
sequentialTasks.description: Fix: copy in osm:ctxutil should pick up new files added to tracked directories. Current _refreshFileItems() only refreshes existing items. Implement directory re-scanning on refresh. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T068: Show summarized parameters in goal list
sequentialTasks.description: goal --list shows name/description/category but not parameters. Add parameter summary. Consider consolidating list logic. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T069: Completion for exec context builder
sequentialTasks.description: exec command in contextManager needs completion support. Explore: shell completion, predefined suggestions, custom completer. Implement within go-prompt REPL. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T070: Git ref completion for diff context builder
sequentialTasks.description: Implement git ref completion for diff command. argCompleters has ['gitref'] — verify and improve. List branches, tags, recent commits. Prefer Go implementation. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T071: Wire osm:flag completion for ctxutil REPL subcommands
sequentialTasks.description: Add argument completion for REPL commands using osm:flag. Wire flag definitions to completion engine. E.g., 'add --recursive' completes '--recursive'. Add tests.

sequentialTasks.task: T072: Hot-shortname aliases with mode-scoped activation
sequentialTasks.description: Add hot-<shortname> aliases that copy snippets per mode. Configurable. Command to output defaults. Warning for unoverridden built-ins (with disable config). Add tests.
Commands must fail on unexpected args/options. Revalidate logging API wiring for all script commands. Consistent error messages for unknown flags. Add tests.
Implement morale-improver goal with post-copy hint for follow-up prompts. Use PostCopyHint field. Create prompt template. Add tests.
Remove tui.createAdvancedPrompt alias from engine_core.go. Update scripts/tests still referencing it. Update documentation.
Run 'make make-all-in-container'. Fix Linux-specific failures. Verify platform code paths (session_linux.go, registry_unix.go, atomic_write.go, filelock_unix.go). Race detection. Zero failures.

Run 'make make-all-run-windows' (host 'moo'). Fix Windows-specific failures. Verify platform paths (session_windows.go, registry_windows.go, atomic_write_windows.go, filelock_windows.go). Zero failures.
Run 'make all'. Verify session_other.go paths, clipboard ops, PTY tests, symlink resolution (/private/var/folders). Zero failures.

Update module table (22 modules from register.go). Document each osm: module with functions, params, return types, examples. Cover grpc, flag, fetch, exec. Document args global, shebang, require() resolution. Fix deprecated references.
Update command.md (sync, review-chunks), config.md (sync.*, script.module-paths, log.*, hot-snippets.*), goal.md (Tier 1 goals), tui-api.md (createPrompt), tui-lifecycle.md. All refs current.
Update session lifecycle, storage backends, auto-cleanup config. Add all new config keys from schema.go. Document OSM_* env overrides. Working examples.
Update module structure (fetch, flag, grpc, sync), command pattern, scripting engine (require, EngineOption), data flow, session management. Fix stale descriptions/diagrams.

Empty [Unreleased] despite 20 commits. Add entries for: config schema, osm:flag/fetch/grpc, require system, sync/diff splitter, go-prompt features, tview deprecation, logging/tailing, session cleanup, prompt files, hot-snippets, Anthropic goals, coverage expansions. Keep a Changelog format.

Extend completion_command.go for all shells. Cover: sync subcommands, config key/value, session list/get/purge, log tail/follow, goal names, script names, session IDs, config keys. Test by sourcing. Integration tests.
Run all scripts/ through 'osm script --test'. Fix API change breakages. Update for createPrompt rename. Document interactive-only scripts.
Run 'go list -m -u all' and 'govulncheck'. Update security-fix deps. Run 'go mod tidy'. Verify 'make all' passes. Document held-back deps.
Verify all doc links valid. Check docs/README.md index. Every command/module/config key documented. Fix stale references. Annotate completed items in docs/todo.md.
Run 'make make-all-with-log'. Zero failures. Coverage meets 100% effective target. Final gate.
Run 'make make-all-in-container'. Zero failures. Fix Linux-specific issues. Final gate.
Run 'make make-all-run-windows'. Zero failures. Fix Windows-specific issues. Final gate.

After all tasks done: holistic review, identify next improvements. Workflow engine, plugin system, MCP server, prompt marketplace, performance regression infra, new goals. Add new tasks. List never empty.

continuousVerification.task: Run 'make all' with full logging after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description: Execute 'make make-all-with-log' after: (a) Each code change, (b) Each test addition, (c) Each task completion, (d) Each bug fix.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: You will ALWAYS aim for 100% effective test coverage, and VERIFY test coverage by means of the tooling available to you. You will NOT proceed to the next task until you have achieved 100% effective test coverage for the current task, and have verified that coverage with the tools available to you.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ON TOOLS: Use `config.mk` to create custom targets to run with Make. ALWAYS use custom targets that *limit* the amount of output you receive. For example, piping through tail, with FEW lines output. Prior to tail, pipe to tee. The file ./build.log in the root of the project is gitignored, so use that. That way you can *search* the output. To be clear, timing dependent tests are BANNED. As are those that take too long to run. Testing retries, for example, MUST be done in a way that supports avoiding running afoul of those CRITICAL rules. Abide. OBEY.
